file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive integration test suite for authentication flow
key_components: Complete registration → login → token refresh flow tests
Mock factories for PrismaClient, AuthService, EventService
Test utilities for creating test users with roles and permissions
test_coverage: Full authentication lifecycle (register, verify email, login, refresh tokens)
Permission-based access control in auth context

patterns_discovered.description: Dedicated factory functions for creating complete mock implementations
examples: createIntegrationMockPrisma() - returns full database mock with all methods
createIntegrationMockAuthService() - returns auth service with token store simulation
createIntegrationMockEventService() - tracks emitted events for verification
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reusable, maintainable mock setup across test cases

patterns_discovered.description: Testing complete workflows through sequential steps with setup between each
examples: Register → Email Verify → Login → Token Refresh as single test
Step-by-step mock setup reflecting real database state changes
patterns_discovered.benefit: Validates interactions between multiple services in realistic scenarios

patterns_discovered.description: In-memory Map-based token storage in mock AuthService to simulate real token lifecycle
patterns_discovered.details: Tokens are generated, stored, validated, and invalidated mirroring production behavior

patterns_discovered.description: Tracking and asserting on events emitted during auth operations
Test users created with complete role and permission hierarchies

gotchas_discovered.description: Password verification uses substring matching instead of proper hash validation
gotchas_discovered.impact: May not catch all password validation edge cases in production
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider more sophisticated password hash simulation in mocks

gotchas_discovered.issue: Token store is memory-based in mocks
gotchas_discovered.description: Tokens exist only in test memory, not persisted
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot test token retrieval after mock restart or multi-instance scenarios
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests must be structured to use tokens within same mock instance lifecycle

gotchas_discovered.description: $transaction callback receives empty object instead of actual prisma instance
gotchas_discovered.potential_issue: Tests relying on actual transaction isolation may not catch concurrency issues
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider implementing more realistic transaction mock with proper context

gotchas_discovered.description: Tests create specific user IDs and email formats that must match across test setup
gotchas_discovered.risk: Brittle tests if data format changes
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Use test data factories with randomization

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive integration test suite for authentication flow
achievements: Multi-step flow testing covering complete auth lifecycle
Sophisticated mock implementations with token store simulation
Clear separation of concerns with dedicated mock factories
approach_outcome.test_granularity: Medium - Tests focus on workflows rather than unit-level detail
approach_outcome.documentation_quality: High - Extensive JSDoc comments and test descriptions

recommendations.details: Expand test suite to cover authentication failures: invalid credentials, expired tokens, revoked sessions, permission denied scenarios
Implement more realistic bcrypt-style hash validation to catch password validation edge cases

recommendations.recommendation: Use test data builders/factories with randomization
recommendations.details: Generate unique test data per run to reduce brittleness and improve test reliability
Pass actual mock Prisma instance to transaction callback to better simulate real Prisma behavior and test isolation

recommendations.recommendation: Add concurrency and race condition tests
recommendations.details: Test simultaneous token refresh, session revocation under concurrent access patterns
Document mock architecture, token flow simulation, and how to extend tests for new features
Monitor and optimize mock setup time as test suite grows; consider parallel test execution
